sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id UInt32, values Array(Int32), group_id UInt32) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
SELECT group_id, arrayAvg(values) AS avg_value FROM test_data GROUP BY group_id HAVING EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM test_data WHERE group_id = test_data.group_id AND COUNT(values) > 0);

SELECT group_id, arrayAvg(x -> (x * 2), values) AS avg_doubled FROM test_data WHERE EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM test_data t WHERE t.group_id = test_data.group_id AND arrayAvg(t.values) > 5) GROUP BY group_id;
SELECT EXISTS(SELECT * FROM test_data WHERE arrayAvg(values) > 5), arrayAvg(values) FROM test_data WHERE EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM test_data WHERE group_id = 1) GROUP BY group_id ORDER BY avg_value;

SELECT arrayAvg(values) AS overall_avg, group_id FROM test_data WHERE EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM test_data t WHERE t.group_id = test_data.group_id AND COUNT(t.values) > 1) GROUP BY group_id HAVING overall_avg IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE additional_data (key String, score Float64) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY key;

SELECT DISTINCT key, arrayAvg(x -> CAST(score AS Float64), arrayMap(x -> x * 2, arrayFilter(x -> x IS NOT NULL, score))) FROM additional_data GROUP BY key WHERE EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM additional_data WHERE key = 'b') ORDER BY key;
SELECT count() FROM test_data WHERE EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM additional_data WHERE key = 'c' AND arrayAvg(values) > 3) GROUP BY group_id;
